posts.text:
Offering “peaceful coexistence or eternal confrontation,” North Korea’s emboldened leader Kim Jong Un said he would only restart talks with Washington if the U.S. ends “hostile” policies and accepts Pyongyang’s nuclear status.
wapo.st/4kRRp0g

I will be moderating this book talk on Wednesday evening EST. Register here and join us:
us06web.zoom.us/meeting/regi…

Big news day in Seoul: South Korea's former president, Yoon Suk Yeol, who declared martial law on the democratic nation in late 2024, has been sentenced to life in prison and convicted of leading an insurrection. 
washingtonpost.com/world/202…

Ousted South Korean president Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to life in prison for insurrection 
washingtonpost.com/world/202…

The court rejected prosecutors' request for the most severe punishment -- the death penalty -- saying that there was no evidence that Yoon meticulously planned martial law in advance, and that there appeared to have been an effort to minimize the use of force that night.
Death penalty would have been life imprisonment for all practical purposes, given that South Korea has not carried out an execution since 1997.
The court also considered Yoon’s lifelong career in public service as a prosecutor, and that he is 65 years old.
BREAKING: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has been convicted of insurrection for declaring martial law in December 2024, and has been sentenced to life in prison.
South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol has entered the courtroom for the verdict in his insurrection trial, the most consequential of the criminal charges he faces tied to martial law.

A South Korean court is set to issue its verdict Thursday in the insurrection case against the country’s impeached president, who declared martial law in an alleged power grab in late 2024, and now faces the death penalty or life imprisonment if convicted. washingtonpost.com/world/202…

My latest in @foreignpolicy.com: with a war in the Middle East, the world appears to have missed that North Korea is now officially getting into the space weapons game (with strong incentives to put nukes in space). My analysis: foreignpolicy.com/2026/03/09/n...

Two ships owned by an Iranian company that the U.S. has accused of supplying material to Tehran’s ballistic missile program departed a Chinese chemical-storage port this week laden with cargo and headed for Iran, according to a Washington Post analysis. 
www.washingtonpost.com/investigatio...

Why is the U.S. at war with Iran?

Trump: “They would have had a nuclear weapon within two weeks”

Vance: “I think the president was looking for the long haul”

Rubio: “We knew that there was going to be an Israeli action”

Trump: “If anything, I might have forced Israel’s hand”

wapo.st/4bAOdCj

Capt. Robert A. Lewis, the co-pilot of the Enola Gay, scribbled thoughts in a notebook — including the phrase, “My God what have we done.” His journal is now for sale. 
www.washingtonpost.com/history/2026...

New @myhlee.bsky.social @washingtonpost.com article on DPRK positioning on nuclear deterrence and potential US engagement cites @apln.network event yesterday with Joel Wit - watch here! www.youtube.com/watch?v=2DjH...

Former Washington Post editor now at The Athletic announces hiring of former Washington Post sports desk staff to The Athletic 
www.nytco.com/press/the-at...

A deeply good human and a dear friend. We will miss you so much. www.azcentral.com/story/news/a...
